title: IDApTIK DLC manifest
description: Envelope for every UMS-produced DLC artifact. The game's DLC loader consumes this envelope; payload internals (puzzle files, scenario definitions, wasm modules) are validated by their own schemas or by the engine that owns the payload type.

id.description: Stable lowercase-kebab identifier, unique across installed DLC.

kind.description: What the artifact is to the game. scenario-definition payloads (buildings/levels, e.g. the Exchange House) and actor-pack payloads (archetype + modifier registries) are validated by idaptik-core's typed loaders — the Rust types are the source of truth for payload internals. edit-script payloads (ordered, replayable edit verbs targeting a scenario, produced by the AI-edit engine) are validated by schemas/edit-script.schema.json — UMS owns the payload as the editor authority.

loads.description: Load point hint for the game loader (e.g. on-puzzle-screen, on-scenario-select).

exports.description: Named entry points the payload exposes.

wasm-modules.description: Paths (relative to the manifest) of compiled modules.

format.description: Payload wire-format tag, e.g. idaptik-scenario/1 or idaptik-edit/1. Versioned independently of the manifest.

path.description: Payload file relative to the manifest.

payload.description: Where the typed payload lives for scenario-definition / actor-pack / asset-pack kinds.

guarantees.description: Machine-checkable claims the payload makes (free-form tags backed by verification).

verification.description: Pointers to the evidence for each guarantee (test suites, soundness docs).
